methods.method_name: Nonparametric Kernel Regression for Trend Estimation
methods.description: This method estimates the trend component of a climate variable time series using nonparametric kernel regression. It aims to separate the trend from noise by averaging data points within a time neighborhood, using a smooth kernel function to improve estimation accuracy.

steps.description: Adopt a simple decomposition of a stochastic climate process in discrete time.
steps.searchable_summary: decompose climate time series into trend and noise components

steps.description: Estimate the trend using nonparametric regression or smoothing method by averaging data points within a time neighborhood.
steps.searchable_summary: apply nonparametric kernel regression to estimate trend

steps.description: Construct an error band to measure estimation uncertainty, considering non-normal distributional shape and autocorrelation.
steps.searchable_summary: construct error bands for trend estimation using bootstrap resampling

steps.description: Perform bootstrap resampling by resampling from regression residuals and time blocks of residuals.
steps.searchable_summary: perform moving block bootstrap resampling on residuals

steps.description: Adapt source code from the University of Zurich for kernel estimation with a parabolic kernel function.
steps.searchable_summary: use adapted kernel estimation code with parabolic kernel function

steps.description: Use block length selector and parameter of a first-order autoregression for uneven time spacing.
steps.searchable_summary: select block length and autoregression parameter for uneven time spacing
